name: variables
context: List, inspect, set, and delete GitLab project-level CI/CD variables, including scoped and protected variables
info: This skill covers project-level CI/CD variables in GitLab: list every variable configured for a project, retrieve a specific key, create or update a variable with scope, masking, protection, hidden behavior, and variable type, and delete a variable precisely when multiple scoped copies share the same key. Agents use it to manage deploy secrets, API credentials, environment-specific flags, and other project-scoped configuration that should not live in the repository.
triggers: store a secret in gitlab ci
body:
# GitLab Project Variables

Project-level CI/CD variables are the durable configuration layer for pipelines. They are the right place for environment-specific secrets, deploy credentials, and configuration values that should not be committed to `.gitlab-ci.yml`.

```bash
python ./scripts/manage-variables.py set group/project AWS_ROLE_ARN arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/deploy \
  --environment-scope production \
  --protected

python ./scripts/manage-variables.py list group/project
```

The Variables API allows multiple variables with the same key when `environment_scope` differs, so retrieval, update, and deletion may require a filter. This is the most common source of automation bugs: the agent updates `FOO` but touches the wildcard version instead of the `production` version. Use [[gitlab/ci]] to understand when a pipeline can actually consume the variable and [[gitlab/pipelines]] when you need to compare a stored variable with the values used by a specific run.

sections.title: List variables and retrieve a single key
sections.body: `GET /projects/:id/variables` returns all project variables and exposes metadata such as `protected`, `masked`, `hidden`, `raw`, `environment_scope`, and `variable_type`. `GET /projects/:id/variables/:key` retrieves one variable; if multiple entries share the key, you must pass `filter[environment_scope]` to select the intended one.

sections.title: Create or update variables
sections.body: `POST /projects/:id/variables` creates a variable and `PUT /projects/:id/variables/:key` updates it. Key fields are `value`, `environment_scope`, `protected`, `masked`, `hidden`, `masked_and_hidden`, `raw`, and `variable_type` (`env_var` or `file`). `raw=false` means variable expansion is allowed; `raw=true` treats the value as a literal string.

sections.title: Scoped variables and collisions
sections.body: GitLab allows the same key to exist multiple times with different `environment_scope` values such as `*`, `staging`, and `production`. Automation should always decide whether it wants a wildcard value or an environment-specific value before writing. When deleting or updating a duplicated key, pass the same scope in both the main request and the `filter[environment_scope]` selector to avoid mutating the wrong row.

sections.title: Protection and secrecy
sections.body: Use protected variables for protected branches and tags, masked variables for values that should not appear in logs, and hidden or masked-and-hidden modes when the value should not be exposed back through the UI. YAML-defined variables are not a substitute for secrets. Store secrets here and keep repository variables limited to non-sensitive defaults or descriptions.

scripts.name: Manage GitLab Project Variables
scripts.description: List, retrieve, create, update, and delete GitLab project-level CI/CD variables

GITLAB_TOKEN.description: Preferred API token; use unless OAUTH_TOKEN or CI_JOB_TOKEN is provided instead

CI_JOB_TOKEN.description: Optional CI job token for in-pipeline automation

GITLAB_HTTP_DEBUG.description: Set to 1 to print request details to stderr

depends.reason: Variable management should only run with an authenticated token that can update the target project
Variable usage and precedence only make sense in the context of GitLab CI execution
